item.title: Exploring the genetic correlation between inflammatory bowel disease and psychiatric disorders: insights from genome-wide association studies.
item.description: European archives of psychiatry and clinical neuroscience - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and psychiatric disorders exhibit complex comorbid patterns, with anxiety and depressive disorders being significantly more prevalent in IBD patients compared to th...

item.title: Global trends in the burden of inflammatory bowel disease from 1990 to 2021: socioeconomic and sex-specific disparities with exploratory mendelian randomization analyses.
item.description: Clinical and experimental medicine - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) shows substantial variation in burden across regions and populations worldwide. However, patterns of IBD b3urden across levels of socio-demographic development rema...

item.title: Diverse clinical spectrum of Niemann-Pick C: insights from a single center.
item.description: Journal of pediatric endocrinology & metabolism : JPEM - Niemann-Pick disease type C (NPC) is a lysosomal lipid trafficking disorder characterized by impaired intracellular cholesterol transport, resulting in progressive neurovisceral involvement. While ...

item.title: Oncostatin Level and Tumour Necrosis Factor-Alpha Gene Polymorphism in Predicting Blastocystis Infection in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Acta tropica - This study investigated the prevalence of Blastocystis spp. infection and serum oncostatin M (OSM) levels in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and examined their association with Blast...

item.title: In Silico Identification and Structural Characterization of High-Risk Missense SNVs in the Human IL23R Gene Relevant to Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Genes - IL23R encodes a pivotal component of the IL-23/Th17 signaling axis and represents a validated genetic susceptibility locus for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), psoriasis, and ankylosing spondyliti...
